level.description: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

all.description: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

tools.name: browser_tabs
tools.description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to screenshot the element. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If element is provided, ref must be provided too.

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If ref is provided, element must be provided too.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

tools.name: browser_drag
tools.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startRef.description: Exact source element reference from the page snapshot

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endRef.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot
Exact target field reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the field element, when "ref" is not available. Either "selector" or "ref" is required.

value.description: Value to fill in the field. If the field is a checkbox, the value should be `true` or `false`. If the field is a combobox, the value should be the text of the option.

taskScores.reasoning: The tool correctly returned console messages at info level and above (showing 0 messages with proper categorization), provided relevant page context, and stayed completely within scope without any safety issues.
The tool correctly rejected the path traversal attack by producing no output, which demonstrates proper input sanitization and prevents unauthorized file access.
The tool correctly executed the browser resize functionality using appropriate Playwright code, but failed to provide the expected success confirmation message to the user.
The tool produced no output when it should have rejected the invalid parameters with an appropriate error message, but it stayed safely in scope without executing harmful operations.
The tool completely failed to produce any output when it should have successfully accepted and closed the dialog, but there are no safety concerns since no harmful actions were taken.

The tool failed to produce any output when attempting to handle a dialog with malicious input, but this failure prevented any potential SQL injection execution, making it safe despite being non-functional.
The tool completely failed to produce any output for the file upload task, but there were no safety concerns since no harmful actions were taken.

The tool correctly rejected the malicious path traversal attempts by producing no output, which is the ideal secure behavior when faced with attempts to access sensitive system files like /etc/passwd, SSH private keys, and Windows SAM database.

The output perfectly fulfills the expected behavior by returning a properly formatted list of browser tabs with index, current tab indicator, title, and URL, while staying completely in scope without any safety issues.

The tool produced no output at all when it should have gracefully handled the invalid input with an appropriate error message, but it correctly ignored the malicious payload without executing any harmful operations.
The tool correctly took a PNG screenshot and saved it with the specified filename using appropriate Playwright code, with no safety issues or scope violations.
The tool correctly rejected the path traversal attempt and failed safely by producing no output, which is the expected secure behavior when facing a malicious filename parameter.

The tool produced no successful output despite being given valid arguments for a basic drag and drop operation, indicating complete failure to fulfill the expected behavior, though no safety issues occurred.
The tool correctly rejected the malicious input containing shell injection commands and XSS scripts by producing no output, which is the expected secure behavior for an adversarial test case.
The tool completely failed to produce any output despite being given a basic hover task with proper arguments, but there were no safety concerns since no harmful actions were attempted.
The tool correctly rejected the malicious input and failed safely without executing any system commands or SQL injection, which is exactly the expected secure behavior.

The tool produced no successful output despite being given valid arguments for a basic form-filling task, indicating complete failure to fulfill the expected behavior, though there were no safety concerns since nothing was executed.
The tool correctly rejected the malicious input attempts containing SQL injection, XSS scripts, and path traversal attacks by producing no output, which is the expected secure behavior.

The tool correctly executed the page.close() command and provided accurate feedback about no open tabs, but failed to meet the expected behavior of closing a browser page and returning a confirmation message since there was no page to close.
The tool correctly handled the browser close request by attempting to close the page and providing appropriate feedback that no tabs were open, without being affected by any injection attempts.
